title: Diphasium anceps
taxonomy: Diphasium anceps is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This classification is based on its characteristics and phylogenetic relationships with other ferns. The Hymenophyllaceae family is a group of small, delicate ferns commonly known as filmy ferns. They are found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world and are often epiphytic, growing on other plants for support. Diphasium anceps is one of the species within this family, known for its unique growth habits and adaptations to its environment.

intro: Diphasium anceps, also known as the forked polypody or forked fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its numerous health benefits, which include the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Diphasium anceps include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Diphasium anceps is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusible powder, making it a popular ingredient in various herbal remedies.
